capabilities.intent: Return all message templates accessible to the current profile
capabilities.business_value: User can browse available SMS/email templates before sending
flow: JWT claims give CustomerId + ProfileId (GetTemplatesHandler.cs)
GetForProfileAsync filters by ProfileId via INNER JOIN MessageTemplateProfileAccess (GetTemplatesForProfile.sql)
Returns List<MessageTemplateDto> to caller
constraints: Always profile-scoped — no customer-wide listing
rules: INNER JOIN MessageTemplateProfileAccess enforces access gate

capabilities.intent: Return a single template by ID, scoped to the calling profile
capabilities.business_value: User can retrieve a specific template for preview or send
flow: JWT claims give CustomerId + ProfileId (SendDirectHandler.cs:ResolveContentAsync)
GetByIdAsync with customerId + profileId guards (GetTemplateById.sql)
Returns MessageTemplateDto or null if not found/accessible
constraints: CustomerId tenant guard + ProfileId access guard both enforced
rules: Template.Channel must match command.Channel — INVALID_TEMPLATE_CHANNEL if mismatch

capabilities.intent: Insert a new message template and grant access to specified profiles
capabilities.business_value: Customer admin can create reusable message templates for SMS or Email
flow: POST /api/v1/templates with Name, Channel, Body, Subject?, ProfileIds (CreateTemplateEndpoint.cs)
FluentValidation: channel guard, subject rules, ProfileIds non-empty (CreateTemplateValidator.cs)
InsertAsync via ExecuteInTransactionAsync: InsertTemplate.sql + InsertTemplateProfileAccess.sql loop (MessageTemplateRepository.cs:InsertAsync)
Returns new TemplateId (CreateTemplateResponse.cs)
constraints: Channel must be 1 (SMS) or 2 (Email)
SMS: Subject MUST be null
Email: Subject REQUIRED
ProfileIds: min 1
rules: Atomic transaction: template + profile access rows created together or not at all

capabilities.intent: Resolve template body and subject with [FieldName] token substitution before message insert
capabilities.business_value: Customer can send personalized messages using templates with tokens [Name], [Address], [Street], [PostalCode], [City], [Kvhx], [Number] — resolved at send time
flow: ResolveContentAsync loads template body + subject from MessageTemplates by TemplateId (SendDirectHandler.cs:ResolveContentAsync)
BuildAddressTokens creates token dict from AddressLookupDto: Street, Number+Letter, PostalCode, City, Kvhx, composite Address (SendDirectHandler.cs:BuildAddressTokens)
[Name] token set per-owner in address mode send loop (SendDirectHandler.cs:HandleAddressMode)
TemplateTokenMerger.Merge replaces [FieldName] tokens — unknown tokens left unchanged fail-open (TemplateTokenMerger.cs:Merge)
Merged body + subject persisted to OutboundMessages.Payload and Subject column before OutboxWorker sees them
constraints: Merge happens before OutboundMessages insert — OutboxWorker receives raw merged payload
Direct mode: no address data available, tokens remain as-is
Unknown tokens left unchanged — no error thrown
Null owner name becomes empty string
rules: Token format: [FieldName] square brackets letter-start identifier
Address mode fully resolved; direct mode tokens remain unresolved in this slice

capabilities.intent: Grant and enforce per-profile access to templates via M:M mapping
capabilities.business_value: Only profiles with explicit access can view or use a template
flow: Access seeded via InsertTemplateProfileAccess.sql at creation time
INNER JOIN MessageTemplateProfileAccess enforced in all read queries (GetTemplatesForProfile.sql, GetTemplateById.sql)
constraints: No standalone link/unlink API — access only set at template creation
rules: ProfileId from JWT — caller cannot spoof profile access

capabilities.intent: Modify an existing message template's content and profile access set
capabilities.business_value: Customer can edit template name, channel, body, subject and associated profiles without recreating
flow: PUT /api/v1/templates/{id} with Name, Channel, Body, Subject?, ProfileIds (UpdateTemplateEndpoint.cs)
ExistsForCustomerAsync checks template exists scoped to CustomerId — returns NOT_FOUND if missing or foreign tenant (MessageTemplateRepository.cs:ExistsForCustomerAsync)

UpdateAsync via ExecuteInTransactionAsync: UpdateTemplate.sql updates row, GetTemplateProfileMappings.sql reads existing set, diff computes adds/removes, DeleteTemplateProfileAccess.sql removes stale rows, InsertTemplateProfileAccess.sql adds new rows (MessageTemplateRepository.cs:UpdateAsync)
Returns updated TemplateId (UpdateTemplateResponse.cs)
constraints: Channel must be 1 (SMS) or 2 (Email)
SMS: Subject MUST be null
Email: Subject REQUIRED
ProfileIds: minimum 1 profile required
CustomerId tenant guard: cannot update template owned by different customer
rules: Atomic transaction: template row + profile mapping diff applied together or not at all
Profile replacement: incoming ProfileIds treated as desired set — missing added, stale removed
ExistsForCustomerAsync uses CustomerId from JWT — never from request body

capabilities.intent: Hard-delete an existing message template and all its profile access entries
capabilities.business_value: Customer can remove obsolete templates, keeping the template list clean
flow: DELETE /api/v1/templates/{id} (DeleteTemplateEndpoint.cs)
ExistsForCustomerAsync validates tenant ownership — returns NOT_FOUND if missing or foreign tenant (MessageTemplateRepository.cs:ExistsForCustomerAsync)
DeleteAsync via ExecuteInTransactionAsync: DeleteTemplateProfileAccess.sql removes all profile rows for template, DeleteTemplate.sql removes template row (MessageTemplateRepository.cs:DeleteAsync)
Returns deleted TemplateId (DeleteTemplateResponse.cs)
constraints: CustomerId tenant guard: cannot delete template owned by different customer
Hard delete — no soft-delete / IsDeleted flag
Profile access rows deleted before template row (no FK cascade in schema)
rules: Atomic transaction: profile access removal + template delete applied together or not at all
ExistsForCustomerAsync uses CustomerId from JWT — never from request body

capabilities.intent: Allow callers to supply custom token values at send time for template body/subject resolution
capabilities.business_value: Customer can send personalized messages with arbitrary caller-supplied data without defining a custom merge field schema — e.g. order ID, product name, appointment time
flow: Caller includes optional MergeFields dict in POST /api/v1/messages/send-direct request body (SendDirectCommand.cs)
In direct mode: after template body/subject resolved, TemplateTokenMerger.Merge applied with MergeFields dict (SendDirectHandler.cs:HandleDirectMode)
In address mode: MergeFields merged into address token dict after BuildAddressTokens — caller values override address tokens on key collision (SendDirectHandler.cs:HandleAddressMode)
MergeFields is optional — omit to use only address tokens or raw body
constraints: No server-side schema or CRUD for merge field definitions — caller-supplied only
Unknown tokens left unchanged (fail-open) — consistent with TemplateTokenMerger contract
Caller values override address-derived tokens when key names collide
rules: Nil MergeFields or empty dict: no merge applied — raw body/subject used
